hypothesis:
Based on the identified gap that only 19 of 31 newly discovered superhydrides (61%) exhibit Tc > 100 K despite all containing 3D hydrogen clathrate structures, I propose:

Hypothesis: The superconducting transition temperature of metal superhydrides is limited not solely by hydrogen clathrate topology but by the degree of metal d-orbital hybridization with hydrogen s-states at interstitial quasi-atom sites, such that engineering metal sublattices with specific d-band filling (between 40-60% occupancy) will systematically elevate Tc above 200 K even in non-clathrate hydrogen networks.
